title: elymus vassiljevii subsp. coeruleus
intro: Elymus vassiljevii subsp. coeruleus, also known as Coeruleus wheat grass, is a subspecies of a perennial grass native to China. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Coeruleus wheat grass include reducing oxidative stress, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antiviral agent, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, Coeruleus wheat grass is often used as a dietary supplement, and its extracts can be found in various forms, including capsules, powders, and teas, allowing for easy incorporation into a healthy lifestyle.
